dependencies: Third-party data center hosting facilities and cloud computing providers
Real-time advertising exchanges and supply-side platforms for inventory access
Third-party data vendors (over 350 integrated as of December 31, 2024)
Dependence on major inventory suppliers including Google
Third-party technology and systems for platform operations
risks: Concentration risk with few inventory suppliers holding significant programmatic inventory
Google is both a major advertising inventory supplier and competitor
Suppliers generally not bound by long-term contracts
Potential disruptions from third-party data center and cloud provider outages
Reliance on third-party APIs and technology that may become unavailable or change terms
Supply chain inefficiencies controlled by inventory suppliers' bidding processes

regulatory_risks: U.S. federal and state privacy legislation including state omnibus consumer privacy laws (California, Virginia, Colorado, etc.)
FTC enforcement under Section 5 of the FTC Act for unfair or deceptive trade practices
EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and UK GDPR compliance
ePrivacy Directive and proposed ePrivacy Regulation in EU
Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) updates
State laws restricting targeted advertising to minors under 18
Washington's My Health My Data Act (MHMD) regarding health data
Cross-border data transfer restrictions (EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework challenges)
Data residency and localization requirements in various jurisdictions
Political advertising transparency and disclosure requirements
Anti-bribery and anti-corruption laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, UK Bribery Act 2010)
U.S. export control and economic sanctions regulations
OECD Pillar Two Model Rules (global minimum tax of 15%)
Digital services taxes in various jurisdictions
Browser and device-level privacy controls (cookie deprecation, IDFA restrictions)
Global Privacy Control (GPC) and similar opt-out signal requirements
IAB Europe Transparency and Control Framework (TCF) viability under review

sanction_risks: U.S. economic sanctions and export controls restricting business with sanctioned countries and entities
Restrictions on doing business with countries, governments and persons targeted by U.S. sanctions
Potential violations leading to criminal/civil fines and reputational harm
Risk of inadvertent business with sanctioned parties despite preventive measures
Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) compliance requirements
